title: Congressional Democrats try to force a vote to end Medicare AI prior authorization pilot
summary: Congressional Democrats are mounting a fresh effort to end a Medicare experiment to use AI to approve or deny care.

title: STAT+: Pioneering trial for treating genetic disease before birth nears reality
summary: A UCSF team has submitted an application to the FDA seeking approval for a small trial of in utero gene therapy for a rare lysosomal storage disorder.

title: Surgeon general’s office issues warning on screen time for children
summary: A public health advisory calls on parents, schools, and government to limit children's use of screens, saying some patterns of use “can pose real harm to children.”

title: STAT+: Florida hospitals win $8 billion in extra Medicaid funds
summary: Stricter limits on extra Medicaid funds are coming, stemming from President Trump's tax cut bill.

title: STAT+: Scientists track cellular disruptions that lead to type 1 diabetes
summary: Growing evidence suggests it takes a sustained viral infection in the right genetic environment to lead to type 1 diabetes.

title: Opinion: I saw Ebola as both doctor and patient. I wish people cared more about the Africans I worked alongside
summary: “As another Ebola outbreak unfolds, we cannot repeat the mistakes of the past,” writes Krutika Kuppalli.

title: STAT+: Biotech execs, academic expert lament impact of FDA turnover on rare disease drug development
summary: How the ouster of key officials at the FDA has created anxiety and uncertainty for the developers of rare disease drugs.

title: STAT+: Kennedy fires two leaders on preventive services task force
summary: Kennedy will have broad latitude to remake the panel, which advises the government on types of preventive care that should be covered by health insurance.

title: After raising $800M, Parabilis seeks an IPO to pursue ‘undruggable’ targets
summary: Parabilis is teeing up the latest high-profile stock issuance in a year when biotech IPOs are averaging more than $286 million in total proceeds.

title: Bristol Myers deepens AI investment with Anthropic deal
summary: The collaboration marks an “evolution” in the pharmaceutical giant’s use of AI and adds to a series of broad deals its peers have used to adopt the technology across many day-to-day activities.

title: Oorja, run by Acceleron veterans, launches to make new fibrosis drugs
summary: The biotechnology startup emerged from stealth with a peptide drug it claims to have the potential to reverse lung scarring in people with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

title: STAT+: Drugmakers guard IP more tightly amid China competition
summary: The FDA's new path, the status of animal testing, and other biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Kennedy names interim surgeon general amid vacancies atop health department
summary: Stephanie Haridopolos, a family medicine physician and senior HHS adviser, will take on the surgeon general's duties on an interim basis.

title: Immunovant’s potential autoimmune ‘blockbuster’; Wave’s RNA editing update
summary: Immunovant shares climbed on results an analyst deemed “compelling” in tough-to-treat arthritis. Elsewhere, Wave’s pioneering rare disease program advanced and Takeda lost an antitrust suit.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about biotech investors’ mood, a former FDA head’s confidence, and more
summary: For Wall Street, the political risk of investing in drug companies has been demoted in comparison to other concerns

title: FDA Warns Retailers Selling Illegal Tobacco Products That Look Like Everyday Products
summary: FDA Warns Retailers Selling Illegal Tobacco Products That Look Like Everyday Products

title: There’s a leadership vacuum at NIH, too
summary: USAID cuts have painful outcomes for the Ebola outbreak, states sue over a new professional ed loan rule, and other health news

title: After a critique from Rubio, WHO defends work on Ebola response
summary: The WHO defended its Ebola response, noting it's national authorities who detect outbreaks. Marco Rubio had said the WHO was "a little late."

title: STAT+: Senate Democrats propose adding long-term care benefit to Medicare
summary: It's part of Democrats' strategy to focus on health care affordability and access in drawing contrasts with Republicans ahead of the midterms.

title: STAT+: NIH behind in filling top roles, with 15 of 27 institutes led by acting directors
summary: An NIH leadership vacuum has persisted for months, with 15 of the 27 institutes being led by acting directors.

title: STAT+: OpenEvidence makes its pitch to hospitals. ‘We’re not crazy monsters’
summary: OpenEvidence CTO Zachary Ziegler said, at the STAT Breakthrough Summit West, that the company is looking beyond physician users.

title: Opinion: The rise of perimenopause misinformation
summary: Are too many symptoms blamed on perimenopause? Two experts weigh in on the “First Opinion Podcast.”

title: STAT+: 23andMe offers to connect users’ DNA data with medical records
summary: 23andMe is partnering with HealthEx to allow users to import their medical records, the company said Tuesday at STAT Breakthrough Summit West.

title: FDA approves first-in-class hypertension drug
Twenty years of induced pluripotent stem cells
STAT+: Marc Tessier-Lavigne addresses new book’s allegations about his conduct in Stanford misconduct case
summary: Former Stanford President Marc Tessier-Lavigne responded publicly for the first time, at a STAT event Tuesday, to allegations in a new book about why he was forced to resign.

title: Trump’s cuts to foreign aid are undermining the Ebola response, insiders say
summary: President Trump's cuts to foreign aid probably hampered the detection of the Ebola outbreak and the response to it.

title: BioMarin drug acquired in buyout misses goal in rare disease study
summary: Tested in a condition known as ENPP1 deficiency, the treatment failed a trial goal that’s important to regulators, spurring doubts about its approval prospects.

title: A migraine drugmaker heads to the public markets through reverse merger
summary: Mentari Therapeutics, a spinout from the prolific hub-and-spoke biotech Paragon, is developing two medications it hopes can treat migraines that aren’t well controlled by existing drugs.

title: Relay drug shows early promise against rare blood vessel diseases
summary: While early, the findings suggest the therapy could be superior to Novartis’ Vijoice at treating “vascular anomalies,” a cluster of chronic conditions with few available treatments.

title: Realizing the potential of agonistic antibody immunotherapy
FDA approves AstraZeneca’s new kind of hypertension drug
summary: Baxfendy inhibits a blood-pressure-raising enzyme and, according to AstraZeneca executives, is poised to be a “big product” that should ultimately generate $5 billion or more in annual sales.

title: Merck ADC, licensed from China, hits mark in first big global trial
summary: The endometrial cancer trial is the first of 17 late-stage studies Merck is running for a therapy that’s important to its ambitions of hitting $70 billion in annual sales next decade.

title: Høeg fired in latest FDA shakeup; 20 people die after taking Amgen drug
summary: The agency has once again shuffled the leadership for two top drug review offices. Elsewhere, Parabilis partnered with Regeneron and an obesity pill is headed toward an approval in China.

title: Autophagy modulation in cancer
